# A voter goes down. The leader engages the witness to maintain commit quorum.
# The leader then crashes and re-campaigns, winning with the witness' help.
#
# Two voters, one witness. Quorum = 2.

setup
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
w
----
v1: [a1] term=a
v2: [a1] term=a

# v1 wins election. Witness vote is dropped to keep setup minimal.
campaign v1 drop=(w)
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2: yes
w:  (dropped)
outcome: won

# v2 goes down (partitioned from here on).
set-avail down=(v2)
----
ok

# v1 proposes an entry.
# Matches: [v1=3, v2=0, w=0]. Quorum match = 0, not committed.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through b3
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# v1 engages the witness. Now it counts toward quorum.
# Matches: [v1=3, v2=0, w=3]. Quorum match = 3, committed!
engage v1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b3, engaged)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b committed=b3 leader

# v2 comes back, but v1 goes down.
set-avail up=(v2) down=(v1)
----
ok

# v2 prevotes (v1 partitioned away). The witness rejects: v2's last log entry
# a1 is from term a, which is not ahead of the witness' vote term b. Without
# proof that a post-engagement leader was elected, granting a vote could let v2
# form a quorum that misses entries the witness helped commit.
prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:  no
outcome: lost

# v1 comes back up, v2 goes back down. v1 could continue using the witness
# (nothing in the state has changed since it last did) but for extra spice,
# let's say it power cycled and lost leadership.
set-avail up=(v1) down=(v2)
----
ok

# v1 re-campaigns. Even though the witness is still engaged, it recognizes v1
# as the leader it supported and grants the vote.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term c
v1: yes (self)
v2: (dropped)
w:  yes
outcome: won

# Nobody to append to, i.e. this is a no-op.
append v1
----
v1 appends through c4
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4] term=c committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# v2 comes back, v1 goes down again. The witness is already disengaged (the
# vote at term c reset the acked state), so v2's prevote succeeds — the witness
# has no engagement to protect.
set-avail up=(v2) down=(v1)
----
ok

prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:  yes
outcome: won

set-avail up=(v1)
----
ok

# v1 proposes and replicates to v2, committing via the traditional v1+v2
# quorum — no witness engagement needed.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through c5
v2: ok
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c committed=c5 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c
